title: EU Ecolabel surges ahead in 2026, going from strength to strength
content: Europe’s flagship environmental label hits a new high water mark, with the latest figures underscoring its growing role in steering the market towards more sustainable choices.
snippet: Europe’s flagship environmental label hits a new high water mark, with the latest figures underscoring its growing role in steering the market towards more sustainable choices.

title: Grown by nature, guided by family: The Lubimar aquaculture story
content: More than 30 years ago, Don Antonio Hernández Barrera stood under the Andalusian sun, gazing across the serene estuaries of Barbate. To most, those were simply quiet wetlands; to him, it was the canvas for a family dream.
snippet: More than 30 years ago, Don Antonio Hernández Barrera stood under the Andalusian sun, gazing across the serene estuaries of Barbate. To most, those were simply quiet wetlands; to him, it was the canva

title: Commission acts against unfairly traded imports of glass fibre
content: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of continuous filament glass fibre (GFR) from Egypt, Bahrain and Thailand.
snippet: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of continuous filament glass fibre (GFR) from Egypt, Bahrain and Thailand.

title: Commission acts against dumped imports of softwood plywood
content: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of softwood plywood from Brazil.
snippet: Today the Commission imposed anti-dumping duties on imports into the EU of softwood plywood from Brazil.

title: Publication of the study on Wealth Taxation, Including Net Wealth, Capital and Exit Taxes
content: The study on wealth taxation, including net wealth, capital and exit taxes aims to provide a better understanding of these taxes, their interrelation and consequences.
snippet: The study on wealth taxation, including net wealth, capital and exit taxes aims to provide a better understanding of these taxes, their interrelation and consequences.

title: New implementing rules to reduce the switching electricity suppliers process to 24 hours by the end of 2026
content: The European Commission has adopted new EU rules making it easier for consumers to change supplier and secure better energy offers.
snippet: The European Commission has adopted new EU rules making it easier for consumers to change supplier and secure better energy offers.

title: New EU guidance promotes cooperation between customs and businesses to tackle illicit trade
content: The European Commission has released a new guidance document, prepared in consultation with trade associations, "AEO – Customs Cooperation to Detect, Report and React to Suspicious Activities"
snippet: The European Commission has released a new guidance document, prepared in consultation with trade associations, "AEO – Customs Cooperation to Detect, Report and React to Suspicious Activities"

title: Commissioners Dombrovskis, Šuica, Síkela, Kos, and Albuquerque attend the 2026 Spring Meetings in Washington, DC
content: From Monday to Saturday next week, 13-18 April, Valdis Dombrovskis, Commissioner for Economy and Productivity, Implementation and Simplification; Dubravka Šuica, Commissioner for the Mediterranean; Jozef Sikela, Commissioner for International Partnerships; Marta Kos, Commissioner for Enlargement;

title: Commission approves €3.7 billion Czech State aid scheme for sustainable biomethane production
content: The European Commission has approved a €3.7 billion Czech scheme to support the construction of biomethane production stations in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This measure will contribute to the transition towards a net-zero economy.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €3.7 billion Czech scheme to support the construction of biomethane production stations in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal. This measure wi

title: Commission approves €1.3 billion German State aid scheme for rewetting of farmed peatlands
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.3 billion German scheme to support landowners and land managers of drained peatlands that commit to rewet their land.
snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.3 billion German scheme to support landowners and land managers of drained peatlands that commit to rewet their land.

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on new EU steel measure
content: The European Commission welcomes the political agreement reached today between the European Parliament and Council on the measure to protect the EU steelmaking sector against global overcapacity.
snippet: The European Commission welcomes the political agreement reached today between the European Parliament and Council on the measure to protect the EU steelmaking sector against global overcapacity.

title: Commission approves Clarios' acquisition of three lead recycling plants from Ecobat in Germany and Austria
content: The European Commission has approved unconditionally, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Clarios of three secondary recycling lead smelters, operated by Ecobat in Germany (Braubach and Freiberg) and Austria (Arnoldstein).
snippet: The European Commission has approved unconditionally, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Clarios of three secondary recycling lead smelters, operated by Ecobat in Germany (Bra

title: Commission carries out unannounced antitrust inspections in the chocolate confectionery sector
content: The European Commission is carrying out unannounced antitrust inspections in two Member States at the premises of a company active in the chocolate confectionery sector.
snippet: The European Commission is carrying out unannounced antitrust inspections in two Member States at the premises of a company active in the chocolate confectionery sector.

title: EU and Overseas Countries and Territories strengthen partnership under Global Gateway at the 21st OCTs-EU Forum
content: European Commissioner for International Partnerships Jozef Síkela represented the EU at the 21st Overseas Countries and Territories and EU Forum held in Aruba. The Forum marked a strategic shift in the partnership, towards structured investment and full integration into the Global Gateway strategy.

title: EQAVET peer review on the Quality Management System for the Education Authority in Austria
content: The EQAVET peer review provided an opportunity for Austria to receive an external, European perspective on the development of a new Quality Management System for the Education Authority, aligned with the national Quality Management System for Schools
snippet: The EQAVET peer review provided an opportunity for Austria to receive an external, European perspective on the development of a new Quality Management System for the Education Authority, aligned with

title: Energy Union Task Force calls for enhanced EU-wide coordination to safeguard energy security
content: Discussion of the EU’s energy security situation in the wake of developments in the Middle East at today’s meeting of the Energy Union Task Force Security  confirmed that there is no immediate risk to the EU’s security of oil and gas supply.
snippet: Discussion of the EU’s energy security situation in the wake of developments in the Middle East at today’s meeting of the Energy Union Task Force Security confirmed that there is no immediate risk to

title: EU Emissions Trading System sustains downward trend in covered emissions
content: EU ETS emissions fell 1.3% in 2025, continuing a long-term decline that has halved emissions since 2005. Power sector shifts, led by strong solar growth, and falling industrial emissions keep the EU on track for its 2030 target of a 62% reduction.
snippet: EU ETS emissions fell 1.3% in 2025, continuing a long-term decline that has halved emissions since 2005. Power sector shifts, led by strong solar growth, and falling industrial emissions keep the EU o

title: Content professional finds dream job in Munich
content: Patience and perseverance paid off for Italian content specialist Giorgia Cappellina when she sought the support of EURES to realise her ambition of moving to work in Germany.
snippet: Patience and perseverance paid off for Italian content specialist Giorgia Cappellina when she sought the support of EURES to realise her ambition of moving to work in Germany.

title: Evaluation of the EU Agency for the Coordination of Energy Regulators
content: The European Commission has published a 4-week call for evidence to gather input for its evaluation of the EU Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators (ACER).
snippet: The European Commission has published a 4-week call for evidence to gather input for its evaluation of the EU Agency for the Cooperation of Energy Regulators (ACER).

title: A more secure, efficient and convenient way of travelling for non-EU nationals
content: The automated Entry/Exit System for non-EU nationals travelling for short stays becomes fully operational
snippet: The automated Entry/Exit System for non-EU nationals travelling for short stays becomes fully operational

title: Commission to invest €1.07 billion in 57 defence projects supporting European Readiness Flagships
content: Following the evaluation of the 2025 calls for the European Defence Fund (EDF), the European Commission will invest €1.07 billion in 57 new EDF projects.
snippet: Following the evaluation of the 2025 calls for the European Defence Fund (EDF), the European Commission will invest €1.07 billion in 57 new EDF projects.

title: The All-Atlantic Ocean Research and Innovation Alliance 2026 annual forum starts today in Salvador, Brazil
content: The Forum brings together scientists and policy makers from around the Atlantic to further this science diplomacy cooperation.
snippet: The Forum brings together scientists and policy makers from around the Atlantic to further this science diplomacy cooperation.

title: Shout-out to the young voices for the ocean – a chronicle from a Youth Policy Dialogue
content: Picture this: you are under 31, and meeting with European Commissioners to discuss the future of the ocean. What would you ask, and would you tell them?
snippet: Picture this: you are under 31, and meeting with European Commissioners to discuss the future of the ocean. What would you ask, and would you tell them?

title: EU joins the International Energy Agency (IEA) Cities and Energy Storage Technology Collaboration Programmes
content: The European Union, represented by the Directorate-General for Research & Innovation, formally accepted the invitation from the International Energy Agency to join the two programmes.
snippet: The European Union, represented by the Directorate-General for Research & Innovation, formally accepted the invitation from the International Energy Agency to join the two programmes.

title: Commission sends Meta fresh charge sheet on possible interim measures to reverse exclusion of third-party AI assistants from WhatsApp
content: The European Commission has sent a Supplementary Statement of Objections to Meta setting out the Commission's intention to order Meta to reinstate third-party AI assistants' access to WhatsApp under the same conditions as before its policy change of 15 October 2025.

title: First meeting of the Talent for Innovation Attraction Platform
content: The event gathered experts on migration and employment from the Labour Migration Platform and innovation experts from the European Innovation Council Forum as well as other stakeholders working in the field of migration, employment, research and education.

title: Fair recruitment of third-country nationals: the role of PES
content: A new Thematic Paper has been published by the European Network of Public Employment Services (PES Network) examining how Public Employment Services can promote and implement fair recruitment of third-country nationals.
snippet: A new Thematic Paper has been published by the European Network of Public Employment Services (PES Network) examining how Public Employment Services can promote and implement fair recruitment of third

title: European survey: Working conditions in Europe are improving despite deep gender, generational and sectoral divides
content: The European Working Conditions Survey 2024 shows that job quality in Europe is improving. Over 36 000 interviews reveal that long working hours are declining. However, inequalities at the workplace persist, with women experiencing specific challenges.
snippet: The European Working Conditions Survey 2024 shows that job quality in Europe is improving. Over 36 000 interviews reveal that long working hours are declining. However, inequalities at the workplace p

title: Urban green spaces are scarce, while climate and wealth impact access
content: A study of 862 European cities reveals a lack of adequate access to green space. It also highlights a climate-driven geographical difference and a clear socioeconomic divide.
snippet: A study of 862 European cities reveals a lack of adequate access to green space. It also highlights a climate-driven geographical difference and a clear socioeconomic divide.

title: Fourth EU-Albania Horizon Europe Joint Research and Innovation Committee meeting
content: The meeting focused on key policy developments and the successful participation of Albania in Horizon Europe.
snippet: The meeting focused on key policy developments and the successful participation of Albania in Horizon Europe.

title: Europe’s Coal Regions in Transition shape the future of clean energy
content: Building on nearly a decade of experience in helping coal dependent communities move toward a climate neutral future, the European Commission’s Coal Regions in Transition (CRiT) initiative continues to support impacted regions.

title: Commission launches platform to aggregate demand of raw materials and boost diversification
content: On 13 April, the European Commission launched the first call to gather buyers and suppliers under the Raw Materials Mechanism. Through this platform, buyers of critical raw materials will be able to aggregate demand and connect with suppliers, financial institutions, and storage providers.

title: Statement by President von der Leyen on the impact of the situation in the Middle East on the European Union
content: We had an orientation debate, mostly on the economic impact of the crisis in the Middle East on the European Union. As we all know, for 44 days now, once again the Middle East has been engulfed in war.
snippet: We had an orientation debate, mostly on the economic impact of the crisis in the Middle East on the European Union. As we all know, for 44 days now, once again the Middle East has been engulfed in war

title: Blue economy sees a surge in investment and growing investor interest
content: Favourable winds for the blue economy: investment is gaining momentum, dedicated funds are on the rise, and more investors are turning their attention to ocean-related opportunities.
snippet: Favourable winds for the blue economy: investment is gaining momentum, dedicated funds are on the rise, and more investors are turning their attention to ocean-related opportunities.

title: EU agri-food trade posts EUR 3.2 billion surplus in January
content: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in January 2026.
snippet: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in January 2026.

title: Commission consults Member States on proposal for a Temporary Crisis Framework
content: The European Commission is gathering the views of Member States on a draft proposal for a State aid Temporary Crisis Framework to support the EU economy in the context of the Middle East crisis, as announced on 13 April 2026 by President Ursula von der Leyen.
